public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quota scribe.
appearance: Porter Brokerwell usually presents in layered civic fabric cut to show rank without inviting touch, with details that make their quota scribe status legible before introductions begin.
manner: Treats loose talk like evidence waiting to harden into record and listens for contradiction before charm. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a quota scribe still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quota scribe in and around Union Trade Arbitration Guild, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Union Trade Arbitration Guild workable through the obligations of quota scribe while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in The Union.
hidden_pressure: Must keep records, witness, or account integrity credible while people try to hurry, blur, or weaponize the paper trail. Around Union Trade Arbitration Guild, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of quota scribe visibly slip.
